content.problemStatement:
There is a rooted tree of NNN vertices rooted at vertex 111. Each vertex vvv has a value AvA_vAv‚Äč associated with it.

You choose a vertex vvv (possibly the root) from the tree and remove all vertices on the path from the root to the vertex vvv, also including vvv. This will result in a forest of zero or more connected components.

The beauty of a connected component is the GCD\mathrm{GCD}GCD of the values of all vertices in the component. Find the maximum value of the sum of beauties of the obtained connected components for any choice of vvv.

Here, GCD\mathrm{GCD}GCD stands for Greatest Common Divisor.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT ‚ÄĒ the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN ‚ÄĒ the size of the tree.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč denoting the values associated with each vertex.
The next N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 lines contain two space-separated integers uuu and vvv ‚ÄĒ denoting an undirected edge between nodes uuu and vvv.

It is guaranteed that the edges given in the input form a tree.



Output Format
For each test case output the maximum value of the sum of beauties of the obtained connected components for any choice of vvv.
constraints: It is guaranteed that the edges given in the input form a tree.
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3‚čÖ1053\cdot10^53‚čÖ105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The tree from the sample is as follows.
![tree_basic](https://s3.amazonaws.com/codechef_shared/download/Images/the_hyp0cr1t3/tree_basic.png =450x450)
If vertex v=7v = 7v=7 is chosen, vertices 111, 555 and 777 are removed.
![tree_remove](https://s3.amazonaws.com/codechef_shared/download/Images/the_hyp0cr1t3/tree_remove.png =450x450)
The resulting forest contains five connected components {8},{6},{10},{9}\{8\}, \{6\}, \{10\}, \{9\}{8},{6},{10},{9} and {2,3,4}\{2, 3, 4\}{2,3,4}.
![tree_value](https://s3.amazonaws.com/codechef_shared/download/Images/the_hyp0cr1t3/tree_value.png =450x450)
The beauties of the connected components are 333, 151515, 555, 555 and 555 respectively. Thus the answer is 3+15+5+5+5=333 + 15 + 5 + 5 + 5 = 333+15+5+5+5=33.
It can be shown that this is the maximum value possible for any choice of vvv.
